ogTitle: Tanglewood vs Tanilba Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tanglewood and Tanilba Bay.
heading: Tanglewood vs Tanilba Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tanglewood, New South Wales and Tanilba Bay, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tanglewood has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Tanilba Bay (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tanglewood or Tanilba Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tanglewood has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Tanilba Bay scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tanglewood scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Tanilba Bay scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tanglewood or Tanilba Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tanglewood has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
